originalTitle: 'I buried my parents one day after the other' - Ebola mourners learn how to grieve safely
title: Being Brave and Safe: How We Say Goodbye When Ebola is Around
content:
Hi everyone! It's your friendly reporter, Kojo, here in Africa! I'm going to tell you about something very important and a little bit sad, but also about how brave and smart people are being. It's about a sickness called Ebola and how communities are learning new ways to stay safe and care for each other.

What is Ebola and Why is it Tricky?

Ebola is a very tricky sickness, friends. It makes people feel very, very unwell. Imagine having a bad fever, feeling super tired, and having a tummy ache, but much, much worse. It's a serious illness that can make people sick very quickly. *Did you know?* Ebola is named after a river in Africa where it was first discovered many years ago! This germ can spread easily from one person to another. It can spread through touching someone who is sick. It can also spread if you touch the body of someone who has passed away from Ebola. That's why we need to be extra careful and understand how to keep everyone safe. In some places, like where the sickness started (we call that the 'epicentre'), many people have become sick. Sadly, more people are passing away, so the special grounds where people are buried are filling up quickly. This shows how serious the sickness is.

New Ways to Say Goodbye Safely

When someone we love passes away, it's a very sad time. We feel a lot of sadness in our hearts. In many parts of Africa, people have big traditional funerals. This means lots of family members and friends gather together. They often touch the person to say a final goodbye. It's a way we show our love. But when Ebola is around, this way of saying goodbye can be very dangerous. The Ebola germs can still be on the body, even after someone has died. If many people touch the body, the sickness can spread to more families and more friends. This is what we desperately want to stop. We want to keep everyone safe and stop the sickness from spreading even more. So, communities are learning brand new, very important ways to say goodbye. These new ways are called "safe burials." They are very important for protecting everyone in the village or town.

Meet Our Brave Safe Burial Teams

Instead of many people gathering and touching, special helpers come to carry out safe burials. These helpers are very brave people. They wear special suits that cover them from head to toe. It's like a superhero suit, but it's for protecting them from invisible germs! These special clothes are called "PPE," which stands for Personal Protective Equipment. *Did you know?* Doctors and nurses who care for sick people also wear PPE to protect themselves and others! They are true heroes. These special teams gently help to bury the person quickly and safely. They make sure the germs cannot spread to other people. This important work means fewer people get sick in the community. It's a very kind and brave thing they do for all of us.

Grieving Safely and Remembering Loved Ones

It’s very, very hard for families. People are still incredibly sad. They miss their family members a lot. They might want to hug and touch, but they know they cannot do that right now because of the Ebola germs. So, they are learning to "grieve safely." Grieving means feeling and showing your sadness when someone you love is gone. Imagine having to bury your mum and dad one day after the other, like one person sadly shared. That is an enormous sadness. But even through their tears, they are trying to understand and follow the rules to keep others safe. Their community is helping them by being kind and supportive, even from a safe distance. Even if we cannot have a big traditional funeral with lots of touching, we can still remember people. We can share happy stories about them. We can draw pictures that remind us of them. We can light a candle in their memory. We can pray for them. These are all beautiful ways to keep our loved ones in our hearts, even if we cannot touch them right now. *Did you know?* Sharing memories and stories about people helps keep their spirit alive in our hearts and helps us feel connected to them.

What Can Kids Do to Help?

Even as kids, you are super important in helping your community stay safe! The very best thing you can do is wash your hands, wash your hands, wash your hands! Use soap and clean water, and sing a happy song like "Happy Birthday" twice while you wash for at least 20 seconds. This chases away those pesky germs! Try your best not to touch your face – your eyes, nose, or mouth – with unwashed hands. Germs love to sneak in that way! If you ever feel sick, tell a grown-up right away. They can help you. Be kind to your friends and family. If someone is sad, you can listen to them, or draw them a happy picture, or just be there for them from a little distance. Showing kindness is always safe and always helps! *Did you know?* Washing your hands properly is one of the very best ways to stop many different kinds of germs from making you sick, not just Ebola! It's like having a superpower for your health!

Hope for a Healthy Tomorrow

It's a difficult time for some communities where Ebola is spreading, but people are strong and brave. Doctors and nurses are working incredibly hard every single day to help people get better. Scientists are also working tirelessly to find new medicines and ways to fight Ebola and stop it completely. We are all working together to make our communities safe and healthy again. By following these safe rules for saying goodbye and by keeping ourselves clean, we are showing enormous love and care for everyone around us. We are stopping the sickness from spreading and helping our communities heal and grow strong again. Thank you for being smart, caring, and brave, everyone!
summary: In communities where Ebola makes people sick, people are learning new, safe ways to say goodbye to loved ones to stop the sickness from spreading and keep everyone healthy.
whyMatters: This is important because it teaches us how to be safe and kind during a tricky sickness like Ebola, and how we can still remember people we love while protecting our friends and family.

originalTitle: Ex-Nigeria oil minister cleared in UK bribery trial
title: Big News from Nigeria: A Lady and the Oil Story!
content:
Hello, Super Friends! Your Africa News Reporter Here!

Hello, hello, my amazing friends! It's your favorite news reporter for Africa, ready to share a super important story with you today! We're going to talk about a big country in Africa called Nigeria. It's a place with lots of sunshine, happy people, and many, many stories! Today's story is about a lady named Diezani. That's a fun name to say, isn't it? Let's call her Ms. D for short. She used to have a very important job in Nigeria. So let's find out what happened!

Who Was Ms. D and What Did She Do?

Ms. D used to be in charge of something super special for Nigeria: oil! Have you ever heard of oil? It's a thick, black liquid that comes from deep, deep under the ground. Think of it like a special treasure that Nigeria has! Ms. D's job was called the "Oil Minister." That means she helped make sure that Nigeria's oil treasure was used well to help everyone in the country. It's a very big and important job!

What is Oil, Anyway?

Let's talk a little more about this amazing oil! Why is it a treasure? Well, we use oil for so many things every single day. It helps our cars go zoom, zoom, zoom on the roads! It helps make electricity so we can have lights in our homes and watch our favorite cartoons on TV. Oil even helps make plastic for our fun toys and many other things we use. So, you see, oil is super, super important for a country like Nigeria to grow and help its people. *Did you know?* Nigeria is one of the biggest countries in Africa that has lots of oil! Many countries buy oil from Nigeria, which helps Nigeria earn money to build schools, hospitals, and roads for its people. It's like having many wells of black gold!

The Big Question: What Was Ms. D Accused Of?

A little while ago, some people said that Ms. D did something that wasn't very fair or honest. They said she took secret gifts, which might have been money, from some very rich people. These rich people were called "oil tycoons." That's a fancy way of saying they are big bosses who own big companies that work with oil. Taking secret gifts or money for special favors is called "bribery." Imagine if you played a game, and someone gave you a special toy to let them cheat. That wouldn't be fair, right? That's a bit like bribery – it means someone isn't being honest or playing by the rules. So, Ms. D was "accused" of doing this. When someone is accused, it means people *think* you did something wrong, but it hasn't been proven yet. It's like when your little brother says you took his cookie, but you actually didn't! You are accused, but you are not proven to be the cookie-taker yet!

Going to Court and Meeting the Jury!

When someone is accused of something big like this, they go to a special place called a "court." A court is like a big classroom or a big meeting room where people listen to all sides of a story to find out the truth. It's a place where everyone tries to be fair. In the court, there was a special group of regular people, just like your mom or dad, called a "jury." The jury's job is super important! They sit and listen very, very carefully to everything that everyone says. They listen to the people who say Ms. D did something wrong and why they think so. And they also listen to Ms. D and her lawyers, who explain her side of the story and say she did not do it. *Did you know?* A jury is usually made up of 12 ordinary people! They listen to all the clues and stories, and then they talk together very carefully to decide if someone is guilty or not guilty. They use their hearts and minds to make a fair decision!

The Big Decision: Not Guilty!

The jury listened for a very long time. They heard all the stories, looked at all the clues, and thought very, very hard about everything. They wanted to make sure they got it right and were completely fair. Then, after all that listening and thinking, the jury had a very important meeting all by themselves. They talked about everything and made a big decision. And guess what they said? They said, "Not guilty!" Hooray! This means the jury decided that Ms. D did *not* do what she was accused of doing. It means they found her innocent of those specific things. And because of this big decision, Ms. D is now free!

Why Is This Important for All of Us?

This story tells us something super important about fairness and justice in our world. Even when powerful people are accused of big things, everyone gets a chance to tell their story. And a group of fair people, like the jury, helps decide if the accusations are true or not. It shows us that our world, especially in places like Nigeria and other African countries, tries very hard to make sure things are fair for everyone. It's like when you play a game with your friends, and there's a referee or a parent to make sure everyone plays by the rules and that no one cheats. That's what justice is about! *Did you know?* Justice means making sure things are fair and right for everyone! It's like making sure everyone gets a turn on the swing, or that if someone breaks a rule, they are treated fairly.

What Does This Mean for Nigeria?

Nigeria is a wonderful and strong country, and it cares a lot about being fair and honest. Having rules, courts, and juries helps make sure that people who have big jobs use their power carefully and honestly. When people know that justice is working and that everyone is treated fairly, it helps everyone trust each other more. It helps build a stronger, happier country for all the children and grown-ups. This story reminds us that honesty is always the best policy, no matter what! And it shows us that our justice system is there to make sure everyone gets a fair chance. We hope Nigeria continues to be strong and fair for all its wonderful children. Thanks for listening, my super friends! See you next time with more exciting news from Africa!
summary: A former Nigerian oil minister, Ms. D, who was accused of taking secret money from big oil bosses, has been found not guilty by a special group of people called a jury in a court, meaning she is now free.
whyMatters: This story is important because it shows that even people in big jobs get a fair chance to tell their side of the story in court, and that special groups of people called juries work hard to make sure decisions are honest and fair for everyone in the country.

title: Nigerian man jailed for storing human faeces outside his home
content: Neighbours said the stench from the property had become so overwhelming that it was impossible to relax at home.

originalTitle: Netanyahu under pressure in Israel after US-Iran agreement
title: A Big Leader Feels Left Out!
content:
Hello, Friends! Let's Talk About Leaders!

Hey everyone! It's your friendly news reporter for Africa here! Today, we are going to talk about something happening far away, but it's important for us to understand. It's about a leader named Mr. Benjamin Netanyahu. That's a long name, right? He is the leader of a country called Israel. Israel is a country not too far from our big continent, Africa! Sometimes, leaders have big jobs. And sometimes, these big jobs can make them feel a bit worried. Mr. Netanyahu is feeling a little worried right now. Let's find out why!

Who is Mr. Netanyahu?

Mr. Benjamin Netanyahu is like the head teacher of a very big school, but his school is a whole country! He is called the "Prime Minister" of Israel. That's a very important job. A Prime Minister helps his country make plans and stay safe. He makes sure that the people in Israel are happy and looked after. **Did you know?** Israel is a country with lots of history! It has many old buildings and special places. **Did you know?** The capital city of Israel is Jerusalem. It's a very old and special city for many people around the world.

What is an 'Agreement'?

Now, let's talk about a big word: "agreement." An agreement is like a promise. It's when two or more people, or in this case, countries, decide to do something together. Imagine you and your friend promise to play soccer after school. That's an agreement! Countries make agreements about many things. Like sharing water, or trading toys, or even being peaceful. These agreements help countries get along and avoid arguments. They are very important for peace. **Did you know?** Sometimes, agreements are written down on paper so everyone remembers what they promised! This makes it official. **Did you know?** Many countries in Africa also make agreements with each other to help their people live better.

The USA and Iran Talk

There are two other big countries in our story today: the USA and Iran. The USA is a very big country far away, across the ocean. Iran is also a big country, closer to Israel. These two countries, the USA and Iran, were talking together. They were making an important agreement. They were making plans about big things, like how to keep everyone safe. They were trying to be friends and work together. This is a good thing, usually! **Did you know?** The USA is sometimes called America, and it's a really, really big country with lots of different weather, from snowy mountains to sunny beaches! **Did you know?** Iran has very beautiful carpets and yummy food like kebabs! It also has a long history, just like Israel.

Why Mr. Netanyahu Feels Left Out

Here's the tricky part of our story. When the USA and Iran made their agreement, they did not talk to Mr. Netanyahu. They made their plans without telling him or asking for his ideas. This made Mr. Netanyahu feel "cut out." Imagine your friends making a plan to play a game, but they don't tell you. You would feel a bit sad, right? You would feel left out. That's how Mr. Netanyahu feels. He feels like his country, Israel, was not included in the big talk. He thinks that the agreement might make his country less safe. Because he is the leader, he cares very much about his country's safety. It's his most important job!

What is 'Pressure'?

When we say Mr. Netanyahu is "under pressure," what does that mean? It means he is feeling a lot of worry. He has a big problem on his mind. He needs to think very hard about what this agreement means for Israel. He wants to protect his people, and he feels like this new agreement might make things harder for him to do that. Imagine you have a big test at school, and you want to do very well. You might feel a bit of "pressure" to study hard! Leaders often feel pressure because they have to make big decisions that affect many, many people. It's a heavy responsibility. **Did you know?** Feeling pressure is normal when you have a big responsibility, like being a Prime Minister or even a big brother or sister who looks after younger ones!

Why is This Important?

So, why should we know about this? It's important because countries need to talk to each other. Even if countries are far away, what happens there can affect others. When leaders feel left out, it can make them sad or worried. It's always better when everyone talks and shares their ideas. It helps everyone feel safe and helps the world stay peaceful. Leaders like Mr. Netanyahu want what is best for their country. He wants to make sure Israel is safe from any problems that might come from this new agreement. **Did you know?** When leaders of countries meet, they sometimes shake hands and take pictures together to show they are trying to be friends and work things out!

What Happens Next?

So, what will Mr. Netanyahu do now? He will keep working hard for his country. He will try to talk to the USA and other countries to tell them his worries. He wants to make sure Israel is safe, no matter what agreements other countries make. Leaders always try to find solutions to problems. They talk, they meet, they send messages. It's like when you have a disagreement with a friend. You talk about it to make things better. The world is a big place, and it's important for everyone to try and be friends and understand each other. Even when it's hard, leaders try to find a way for peace. They know that talking is the best way to solve problems without fighting. **Did you know?** Sometimes, countries even send special messengers, called diplomats, to talk to each other when leaders can't meet in person! They are like special envoys of peace.

Let's Remember to Talk!

So, that's our story about Mr. Netanyahu feeling left out. It reminds us that talking is very important. It's important for friends, for families, and for countries! When everyone talks, it's easier to understand each other and make sure everyone feels safe and happy. Let's all remember to talk to our friends and family, just like leaders should talk to other countries. Thank you for listening, my dear friends! See you next time for more news from Africa and beyond!
summary: A leader named Mr. Netanyahu in the country of Israel is worried because two other big countries, the USA and Iran, made an important agreement without including him.
whyMatters: It's important because when leaders are left out of big talks, they can feel worried about their country's safety, and talking together helps everyone stay friends and peaceful.

originalTitle: Who benefits from Zimbabwe’s lithium boom?
title: Zimbabwe's Super Secret Rock Treasure!
content:
Hello from Sunny Zimbabwe!

Hi friends! I'm your super news reporter, and I'm so excited to tell you a fascinating story from a beautiful country in Africa called Zimbabwe. It's a land full of amazing animals, like elephants and giraffes, and wonderful, kind people. But guess what? There's also a very special secret treasure hidden deep under the ground in Zimbabwe! We're going to learn all about it today.

What is This Shiny Rock Called Lithium?

Imagine a super special kind of rock that helps make many of your favourite toys and gadgets work. It's called **lithium**! It might not sparkle like a diamond, but it is incredibly important for our world today. Lithium is a very light, silvery-white metal that we can find dug out of the earth. Think about the phone your mom or dad uses, or the remote control for your TV, or even your cool battery-powered toy car. All these things need batteries to work, right? Many of those important batteries are made with lithium! It helps them store power so your gadgets can play and work for a long, long time. Even big, quiet electric cars that don't use noisy petrol need lots of lithium for their powerful batteries. So, lithium helps us make our world cleaner and helps our planet by reducing pollution! It's truly a special rock that powers many of the things we use every single day to learn, play, and connect. Did you know? Lithium is the lightest metal on Earth! It's so light that a piece of it can even float on water. But remember, we should never play with these kinds of metals at home, as they need special care.

Zimbabwe's Big Treasure Chest!

Guess what amazing news? Our friend, the country of Zimbabwe, has discovered that it has huge amounts of this special lithium hidden right there in its ground! It's like finding a giant treasure chest full of precious rocks, just waiting to be carefully taken out. For many years, people didn't know exactly how much lithium Zimbabwe had. But now, clever geologists (they are like rock detectives and scientists!) have found really big amounts. This makes Zimbabwe a very important place in the world for getting lithium. Many other countries are now looking at Zimbabwe with excitement. When a country has a lot of something that the whole world needs, it's a really big deal! It can bring many good things, like new jobs for people, more money for the country to spend on important things, and lots of positive changes. Zimbabwe is now becoming famous for its lithium, and everyone is talking about it. Did you know? Africa has many amazing natural treasures, like gold, diamonds, and many other important minerals. Lithium is one of the newest and most exciting discoveries that helps make our modern world work!

New Friends Helping to Dig!

Imagine you have a really big sandbox, and you want to dig a super deep hole. Sometimes, you need help from your friends, right? You might need bigger shovels or more hands. Well, Zimbabwe is getting help from good friends in other countries around the world. These friends are bringing big, strong machines, smart ideas, and lots of money to help. These helpful friends are investing their money to start new mines. Mines are like very deep, special places where we carefully dig out the rocks that have lithium. It takes a lot of special tools, careful planning, and smart engineers to do this work safely and well. They want to make sure no one gets hurt and that the land is looked after. They are also building new places where the lithium rocks can be cleaned up and made ready to be used in batteries. These new places are called processing plants. They are like big factories where the rocks are transformed. This makes the lithium even more valuable. All these new projects mean that more and more people in Zimbabwe can get jobs. People can learn exciting new skills, like how to drive giant trucks, how to work with special machines, or even how to become engineers. It's a time of learning and growth for many families and communities.

What Good Things Come from Lithium?

When Zimbabwe sells its special lithium to other countries, it gets money in return. This money is like a big piggy bank for the whole country! So, what can Zimbabwe do with this important money to help its people? This money can be used to build fantastic new schools, so even more children can learn, read, and grow up to be amazing doctors, teachers, or even rock scientists themselves! It can also help to build better hospitals and clinics, so people can get healthy care when they are sick or need help. The money can also help build new, smooth roads and strong bridges, making it easier for people to travel safely and for food and other important goods to be moved around the country. This helps everyone, from farmers to families, and makes life more comfortable and connected. More jobs for people also mean that families have money to buy healthy food, comfortable clothes, and other important things they need for their homes. This makes life better for many people and helps communities all over Zimbabwe grow strong and happy. It's all about making life better for everyone.

Making Sure Everyone Shares the Goodness!

Now, this is a very important part of our story. When a country finds a big treasure like lithium, we want to make sure that *everyone* gets a chance to feel the good changes, right? We want to make sure the benefits are shared fairly, like sharing your favourite snacks with all your friends. Sometimes, when very big projects like mines start, some people living nearby might get lots of good things, like new jobs or better houses. But other people in different parts of the country might not feel the good changes as much, or they might even have new challenges that come with big changes. The leaders in Zimbabwe and the helpful friends from other countries are working hard to make sure the good things that come from lithium reach many, many people across the country. They want to make sure that this growth and development is fair and helps as many people as possible. This means thinking carefully about the communities living very close to the new mines. We want to make sure their homes are safe, their water is clean, and they also get good opportunities to work, or maybe even start their own small businesses that can help their towns grow. It’s like ensuring everyone gets a piece of the birthday cake!

A Brighter Future for Zimbabwe!

Zimbabwe's amazing lithium treasure is helping to change things in wonderful ways. It's bringing new hope, new learning, and many new opportunities for people all across the country. People are learning exciting new skills, and the country is getting more money to build important things that help everyone. We all hope that this special rock helps Zimbabwe grow even stronger, even more prosperous, and much happier. We want to see many, many children going to school with smiles on their faces, many families having good homes, and a lot of happy faces all around in this beautiful African country. It's a big and exciting adventure, and Zimbabwe is just at the beginning of its special lithium story. With careful planning, smart thinking, and lots of kindness, this treasure can truly help build a wonderful and bright future for everyone in this amazing country. Did you know? Zimbabwe is also famous for its breathtaking Victoria Falls, which is one of the largest and most spectacular waterfalls in the entire world! It truly is an incredible place, full of wonders, both above and below the ground!
summary: Zimbabwe has discovered a lot of a special rock called lithium, which is used in batteries for phones and cars, bringing new jobs and money to the country, and everyone hopes these good things will be shared fairly to build a brighter future for all.
whyMatters: This is important because finding lots of lithium can bring many good things to Zimbabwe, like new jobs, money for schools and hospitals, and help the country grow, but it's also important to make sure everyone benefits from this special treasure.

content: Around 100 people, including Greta Thunberg and other Flotilla activists, have rallied in Brussels.

title: Big News for Countries Far Away!
content:
Hello, My Super Awesome Friends!

Hello, hello, hello! It’s Sunny, your friendly news reporter, beaming to you right from beautiful Africa! I’ve got some SUPER exciting news today, and even though it’s about countries far, far away, it’s going to make all our hearts happy! Are you ready for some amazing news about peace and making the world a better place? Let’s dive in!

What's This Big News About?

Imagine two big friends, or maybe two big countries, who were having a disagreement. Sometimes, friends argue, right? But then, they talk and talk, and they decide to stop arguing and become friends again. That’s kind of what happened with two very important countries called the United States (we can call them the US for short) and Iran. They made a special promise to each other, a super important agreement! It’s like a secret handshake, but even bigger and better, because it means good things for many, many people. This agreement has three amazing parts, and I can't wait to tell you all about them!

No More Fighting! Yay for Peace!

The first part of their super agreement is the best part: they both said, “NO MORE FIGHTING!” Can you believe it? When big countries fight, it’s called a war, and wars make people very, very sad. Homes can get broken, and people can get hurt. It’s never good. But now, the US and Iran have promised each other to stop the fighting. This is like when you and your friend stop arguing over a toy and decide to share and play nicely again. Isn't that wonderful? It means more peace in the world! **Did you know?** When there is peace, children can go to school every day without worry, and families can live safely in their homes. Peace helps everyone be happy and play! This promise to stop fighting is a huge step. It means that people in these countries can start to feel safer and think about happy things instead of sad things. It's like the sun coming out after a big, rainy cloud. Hurray for peace and friendship!

No More Super Scary Bombs!

Now, for the second BIG, BIG promise in this agreement. This one is super important for keeping everyone safe, everywhere. Iran made a promise that it will never, ever make a super scary thing called a nuclear weapon. Phew! That's a relief! What is a nuclear weapon? Imagine the biggest firecracker you have ever seen, but a million, million times bigger and more dangerous! It’s so powerful that it can make a huge mess and hurt many, many people and places. It’s like a giant, dangerous toy that no one should ever play with, because it’s not safe at all. So, Iran promising not to have one of these scary weapons means the world is a much safer place for you, for me, and for all our friends around the globe. It's a promise to protect everyone and prevent big, bad accidents. This makes everyone breathe a sigh of relief! **Did you know?** Most countries in the world agree that these super big, scary weapons are not good. They want everyone to be safe and healthy, just like your mommy and daddy want you to be safe! This part of the agreement shows that these countries want to be responsible and help keep the world a peaceful and secure place for all children to grow up in. Isn't that amazing?

Lots of Money for Good Things!

And here’s the third amazing part, my wonderful friends! The agreement also means that Iran will get a SUPER, SUPER big amount of money. We’re talking about a mountain of money! It’s called 300 billion dollars! That's like so many shiny coins, you couldn't even count them all in your lifetime! It's more money than you can ever imagine! So, what’s this huge amount of money for? It's for something called 'redevelopment.' That means making things new and better! Imagine building brand new, colourful schools for children, so they can learn and play. Imagine shiny new hospitals to help sick people get well. This money can also help build lovely parks for everyone to play in, better roads so people can travel easily, and even bring clean, fresh water for everyone to drink. It's all about fixing things that were broken and building new things to make life much, much better for all the people living in Iran. **Did you know?** In many parts of Africa, money like this helps build new bridges so people can cross rivers safely, and bring electricity so children can study and read their favourite books at night! This money will help so many people. It means more chances for children to go to school, for families to have clean water, and for everyone to live in a safer, happier place. It's like a big gift that will help a whole country!

Why This News Matters to Us!

So, why is all this super exciting news important for us, even though it’s happening far, far away? Because when big countries agree to be friends, stop fighting, and promise to keep everyone safe, it makes the whole world a happier and brighter place! Everything in the world is connected, like the roots of a big, strong tree. It shows us that talking and sharing is always better than fighting. It means more smiles, more peace, and more help for people to live good lives. It sets a good example for everyone, big and small. When there is peace in the world, we can all dream bigger dreams and work together to make our planet even more wonderful. Isn't that just the best news ever?

Until Next Time!

So there you have it, my wonderful friends! A big agreement that brings peace, safety, and a chance for a better life to many people. It teaches us that even when things are tough, talking nicely and making promises can make everything much, much better. Let’s all remember to be kind and friendly to everyone, just like these big countries are trying to do! This is Sunny, your friendly reporter from Africa, signing off! See you next time with more happy news!
summary: Two big countries, the US and Iran, made a super good agreement to stop fighting, not build scary bombs, and help build a better life for people in Iran with lots of money.
whyMatters: This news is super important because it means more peace and safety for everyone around the world, and lots of help to make a country much, much better, like building new schools, hospitals, and parks for children to enjoy!

title: Fans boo, players adapt - the view on World Cup hydration breaks
content: Supporters are booing them, coaches are using them, players are adapting to them - mid-half hydration breaks are affecting everyone at the World Cup in different ways.

originalTitle: 'Chip on shoulder' may help Bellingham to best form
title: Jude Bellingham: How Big Talk Makes Him Play Super!
content:
Hello, Super Friends!

Hi, super friends! It's your kid-friendly reporter here, bringing you cool news from the amazing world of football! Today, we are talking about a very special football player. His name is Jude Bellingham. Jude is a young man who plays football really, really well. He is from a country called England. And he plays for the England team!

What is the World Cup?

Now, imagine a super big football party! This party happens only every four years. It's called the World Cup. Teams from all over the world come to play in this big tournament. They all want to win a shiny golden cup! It's super exciting and fun to watch. Jude Bellingham's team, England, plays in this big party too. They want to win the cup! Did you know? Football is the most loved sport in the whole wide world! Many, many people play and watch football.

Jude and the England Team

Jude is a very important player for England. He runs a lot on the football field. He helps his team score goals. He helps them stop other teams from scoring too. Jude is a 'midfielder.' That means he plays in the middle part of the field. It's a tough job, but Jude is very good at it. He works very hard for his team. Did you know? The World Cup is so special, it only happens every 4 years! That means players have to wait a long time to play in it again.

What is 'Noise'?

Sometimes, when you are very good at something, people talk about you a lot. They might say good things, or sometimes they might say things that are not so nice. They might talk about how Jude plays. This is what we call 'noise' sometimes. It's like many voices talking about one person. It can be a lot of talk, a lot of different ideas about him.

How 'Noise' Can Help Jude

But guess what? Jude Bellingham is a very smart and strong player! He says that all this 'noise,' all this talking, actually helps him! He feels like it makes him stronger. It makes him want to play even better. He wants to show everyone what he can do! He wants to be his best when he plays. It's like when you try extra hard because you want to show you can do it!

Playing His Very Best

When Jude says he wants to find his 'best form,' it means he wants to play his absolute best game ever. He wants to run faster, kick the ball better, and make super good passes to his friends. He wants to score goals that make everyone cheer loudly! This 'noise' gives him a special power, he thinks. It makes him focus and try extra, extra hard in every game. Did you know? Jude Bellingham is still very young for such a big player! He started playing professional football when he was just 16 years old!

Being Strong Inside

This tells us something important about Jude. He is strong inside! He takes all the talk, even the not-so-nice talk, and turns it into strength. He uses it to become better. Isn't that amazing? We can all learn from Jude. When things are tough, we can use our inner strength. We can try our best and never give up!

Importance of Teamwork

Football is not just about one player, remember. It's about a whole team working together. Jude plays his best, but he also helps his teammates play their best. They pass the ball, they help each other, and they celebrate together! Together, they are much stronger than just one person. Teamwork makes the dream work! Did you know? Many amazing football stars come from Africa, like Samuel Eto'o and Didier Drogba! They inspire many young children to play football.

Dreams Can Come True

Jude Bellingham worked very hard to become such a good player. He trained a lot, almost every day. He loved football so much! Now, he is living his dream, playing for his country in the World Cup! This shows us that if we work hard and believe in ourselves, our dreams can come true too. Keep dreaming big, friends!

A Message For You!

So, my dear friends, remember Jude Bellingham's story. When people talk about you, or when you face a challenge, remember to be strong like Jude. Use that energy to try even harder. Show everyone what a super star you are! Believe in yourself, play fair, and always, always have fun!

Ready for the World Cup!

Jude Bellingham is ready to play his best for England in the World Cup. He is taking all the 'noise' and making it his super power! We wish him and his team good luck. Keep cheering for your favourite teams and players! Bye for now!
summary: Jude Bellingham, a young football star, feels that people talking about him will make him play even better for his England team in the big World Cup.
whyMatters: This story teaches us that even when people talk about us, we can use it to become stronger and try our very best, just like a super football player and achieve our dreams.
